text: During a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, the surgeon repairs an anterior laceration of the cystic duct using a minimally invasive technique. A T-tube is inserted, and a 4-0 PD suture is used to close the duct over the tube. The repair is performed laparoscopically, with visible manipulation of the small anatomical structures.

more time to do this repair I think overall the outcome of the patient was improved without having to open her here we kind of tried to re-approximate the duct using a laparoscopic knot pusher and the duct really came together quite well over this repair after we through our first suture, an additional suture was then put into place. Usually these repairs are done open and many times the repairs are actually not caught until several days later when the patient has become very, very sick developed some signs of bioperitonitis. Luckily, the surgeon operating on this caught the repair early and were able to repair it minimally invasively.

The surgeon demonstrates the small size of the patient's common duct, highlighting the difficulty of inserting the T-tube. Two sutures are used to approximate the edges of the duct. A cholangiogram is performed through the T-tube, showing good bile flow into the duodenum.
